figures.name: fig1_transonic_profiles
figures.issues: Three-panel layout (velocity+sound speed, Mach number, density) is clean. Axes labeled with units (mm/s, 10^6 m^-1, x/xi). Colors now rendering correctly: steel blue for Rb87, berry for K39, amber for Na23. Mach=1 crossing at x=0. All densities positive. Solid vs dashed lines distinguish v(x) from c_s(x).

figures.name: fig2_correction_hierarchy
figures.issues: Grouped bar chart on log y-axis. Hierarchy delta_disp > delta_diss > delta_cross clearly visible. Numeric annotations on bars. Uses green/red/gray for correction types (dispersive/dissipative/cross) which is internally consistent. Sensitivity threshold dashed line present.

figures.name: fig3_parameter_space
figures.issues: 2D contour plot in (log10 D, log10 gamma/kappa) space. Colorbar labeled. EFT boundary at D=1 marked with dashed line. Three experimental star markers labeled. Marker labels are small but legible on close inspection.

figures.name: fig4_spin_sonic_enhancement
figures.issues: Log-log plot showing quadratic enhancement. Slope approximately 2, confirming delta_diss ~ (c_dens/c_spin)^2. Three annotated experimental points. Shaded region below curve. Monotonically increasing.

figures.name: fig5_temperature_decomposition
figures.issues: Three-panel bar charts. T_H dominates by 6+ orders of magnitude, making correction bars nearly invisible even on log scale. The delta_disp and delta_cross bars appear as hairlines in Heidelberg and Trento panels. Consider inset or separate correction-only subplot to make the decomposition readable.

figures.name: fig6_kappa_scaling
figures.issues: Previously flagged for x-axis extending to 10^198. Now fixed: x-axis runs from ~10 to ~10k s^-1, a physically reasonable range. delta_disp ~ kappa^2 (green, rising) crosses delta_diss ~ 1/kappa (red, falling) at kappa* ~221 s^-1, clearly marked with X. Experimental kappa values shown as vertical reference lines (steel blue, berry, amber). Well-rendered.

figures.name: fig7_cgl_fdr_pattern
figures.issues: Grouped bar chart for monomial counting at EFT orders N=0..6. Four categories clearly distinguished. count(N) = floor((N+1)/2)+1 dashed line overlay matches. Noise bilinears at even orders only. Clean.

figures.name: fig8_even_vs_odd_kernel
figures.issues: Four-panel pedagogical figure. (a) Even-omega K_R symmetric parabola. (b) Odd-omega linear/antisymmetric. (c) Noise kernel from FDR is constant. (d) Summary: even->0 noise, odd->finite noise. Green/red color coding consistent. Excellent layout.

figures.name: fig9_boundary_term_suppression
figures.issues: IBP correction vs D on log-log axes. Linear scaling confirmed (slope ~1). All experiments at D~0.01, well within 2% threshold. EFT breakdown at D=1 clearly marked. Experimental labels slightly clustered but readable.

figures.name: fig10_positivity_constraint
figures.issues: gamma_{2,1} vs gamma_{2,2} constraint space. Strict line (slope -1) through origin. Relaxed bound as parallel bands. Allowed (green) and forbidden (red) regions. Clean geometric layout.

figures.name: fig11_on_shell_vanishing
figures.issues: Key physics result: delta^(2) crosses zero precisely at k/k_acoustic=1. Negative below, positive above. Dashed vertical line at acoustic shell. Shaded near-shell band. Near-shell suppression annotated (~10^-7). Crisp zero crossing.

figures.name: fig12_einstein_relation
figures.issues: sigma = gamma*T for three gamma values (0.5, 1.0, 2.0). All lines through origin, linear. Correct slope ordering. CGL master formula annotation box. Clean.

figures.name: fig13_parity_alternation
figures.issues: Heatmap showing monomial structure at EFT orders N=1..8 vs time derivatives m. Cells labeled with (N-m, m) indices. Red (flow-only/parity-breaking) and green (universal/parity-preserving) color coding. The red/green scheme may be problematic for color-blind readers. Gray cells for m>N could use explicit 'forbidden' labels. Physics content (alternation pattern) is correct.

figures.name: fig14_parity_alternation_stakeholder
figures.issues: Bar chart of transport coefficient counts per order N=1..8. Red (flow-only) and green (universal) bars with dashed count(N) formula line. Same red/green color-blind accessibility concern as fig13. Consider replacing with blue/orange for accessibility. Physics content correct: alternation between universal (odd N) and flow-only (even N) structures visible.

figures.name: fig15_damping_rate_third_order
figures.issues: CONVERGENCE NOT VISIBLE. Three EFT order curves (order 1 dashed, order 2 dotted, order 3 solid) overlap completely and appear as a single curve across the entire k range. The figure's purpose is to demonstrate EFT convergence by showing three distinct curves that approach each other, but no distinction is visible. Either: (a) the traces are computed identically due to a bug, (b) convergence is so rapid that differences are sub-pixel, or (c) line styles are not rendering distinctly. Needs a residual/difference inset panel or zoomed view near k/k_H=1 to show order-by-order differences.

figures.name: fig16_spectral_correction_comparison
figures.issues: LEFT PANEL HAS NUMERICAL NOISE. The delta^(2)(omega) plot (intended to show odd-omega symmetry) is dominated by rapid oscillations at the ~6e-13 level, which is floating-point noise, not physics. The intended odd-symmetry structure is completely obscured. The right panel (delta^(3), even-omega) is clean and shows the expected symmetric parabolic shape. The left panel needs: either higher-precision numerics (mpmath), analytical evaluation, or -- if the vanishing IS the point -- explicit labeling showing that delta^(2) is zero to machine precision on-shell, with the y-axis scale contextualized against delta^(3).

figures.name: fig17_kappa_crossing
figures.issues: Extended kappa-crossing for all three platforms with 6 traces. Physics correct: all platforms show dispersive/dissipative crossings with diamond markers at crossing points. However, all curves are rendered in black/dark monochrome with only solid vs dashed line-style differentiation. The manifest specifies Steinhauer/Heidelberg/Trento color keys but the project palette (steel blue/berry/amber) is not used. Distinguishing the three platforms requires careful legend reading. Should use platform-specific colors.

figures.name: fig18_spin_sonic_phase3
figures.issues: Spin-sonic enhancement for all three platforms on log-log axes. Three parallel lines with correct quadratic slope. Platform ordering correct (different y-intercepts reflect different base dissipation rates). However, all three curves rendered in black/dark monochrome rather than using project palette (steel blue/berry/amber). Legend is readable but visual differentiation is poor. Should use platform-specific colors.

figures.name: fig19_bogoliubov_connection
figures.issues: Two-panel figure. (a) Dispersion relation: acoustic linear (dashed) vs Bogoliubov supralinear (solid blue) clearly diverge for k*xi > 1, with fractional deviation curve (red). (b) k^4 deviation: exact deviation (red) matches (k*xi)^4/8 guide (dotted) at small k, then grows faster. Physics correct: BEC dispersion is superluminal. Units (c_s*xi, k*xi) dimensionless and correct.

figures.name: fig20_sm_scorecard
figures.issues: SM gauge erasure scorecard. Three bars: SU(3)_c (dim 8, red/ERASED), SU(2)_L (dim 3, red/ERASED), U(1)_Y (dim 1, green/SURVIVES). Bar heights match group dimensions. Clear annotations with Casimir labels. Red/green coding for erased/survives is appropriate here since it maps to a binary pass/fail status.

figures.name: fig21_erasure_survey
figures.issues: Extended erasure survey: SU(2)..SU(10), SO(3)..SO(10), U(1). All non-abelian groups marked 'Erased' (red), only U(1) 'Survives' (green). Bar heights proportional to group dimension. Vertical dashed separators between SU(N)/SO(N)/U(1) sections. Minor issue: x-axis labels for SO groups are slightly crowded at smaller display sizes. The SU(N)=SO(N) and SO(N)=U(1) boundary annotations at top are small. Overall physics is correct and the universal erasure pattern is clear.

figures.name: fig22_complex_turning_point
figures.issues: Imaginary turning point shift x_imag vs omega/T_H for three platforms. Correct project palette: steel blue (Steinhauer), berry (Heidelberg), amber (Trento). Log y-axis. All curves monotonically increasing with frequency. Steinhauer and Heidelberg closely spaced, Trento well separated below (smaller dissipation). Clean labels, legend present.

figures.name: fig23_effective_surface_gravity
figures.issues: Three-panel decomposition (Steinhauer/Heidelberg/Trento). Each panel shows delta_disp (green, flat/frequency-independent) and delta_diss (red, growing with omega). Physics correct: dispersive correction is constant in frequency while dissipative grows. Two issues: (1) Only Steinhauer panel has a legend; Heidelberg and Trento panels lack legends. (2) Green/red colors for dispersive/dissipative do not use project palette. Y-axis scales differ between panels without explicit indication.

figures.name: fig24_decoherence_and_noise
figures.issues: Two-panel figure. (a) Decoherence parameter delta_k vs omega/T_H. (b) FDR noise floor n_noise vs omega/T_H. Both panels use correct project palette: steel blue (Steinhauer), berry (Heidelberg), amber (Trento). Log y-axes. Curves monotonically increasing. Platform ordering consistent between panels. Legend present in both. Clean.

figures.name: fig25_hawking_spectrum_exact
figures.issues: Three vertically stacked panels (Steinhauer/Heidelberg/Trento). Each shows n_total (solid), n_planck (dashed), n_noise (dotted) on log y-axis vs omega/T_H. Spectra are thermal (Planckian) at low omega with noise floor visible at high omega. Platform-specific colors used. n_total >= n_planck everywhere (noise adds). The hierarchy between Planckian signal and noise floor is clear.

figures.name: fig26_hawking_spectrum_stakeholder
figures.issues: Overlay of all three platforms' total n(omega) on single log-log plot. Correct palette: steel blue (Steinhauer), berry (Heidelberg), amber (Trento). Spectra nearly identical at low omega (universal thermal regime), splitting at high omega (platform-dependent corrections). Clear legend. Publication-ready stakeholder summary.

figures.name: fig27_exact_vs_perturbative
figures.issues: Fractional difference (n_exact - n_pert)/n_pert vs omega/T_H. Three platforms with correct palette (steel blue/berry/amber). Near zero at low omega (perturbative valid), growing at high omega (EFT breakdown). Steinhauer and Heidelberg diverge for omega > 5*T_H; Trento stays small. Physically correct: larger corrections for platforms with larger adiabaticity parameter D. The '% lev' annotation for Trento at high omega is slightly small but readable.

summary.overview: 27 figures reviewed: 17 PASS, 8 MINOR, 2 FAIL. Phase 1-2 figures (fig1-12) are solid after prior fixes (fig6 axis range now correct). Phase 3 figures (fig13-27) are mostly clean with two requiring attention.

critical_fixes_needed.issue: All three EFT order curves (1, 2, 3) overlap completely -- convergence is not demonstrable from this plot. Either a data/trace bug or the differences are sub-pixel at this scale.
critical_fixes_needed.suggested_fix: Add a second panel showing fractional difference between successive orders: |Gamma_N - Gamma_{N-1}| / Gamma_N. Alternatively, add a zoomed inset near k/k_H ~ 1 where order-by-order differences should be most visible. Verify in the code that three distinct data arrays are actually being plotted.

critical_fixes_needed.issue: Left panel (delta^(2) odd-omega) is pure floating-point noise at ~6e-13 level. The intended odd-symmetry demonstration is completely obscured.
critical_fixes_needed.suggested_fix: Three options: (1) Compute delta^(2)(omega) with mpmath at higher precision. (2) If the vanishing IS the intended result (delta^(2) = 0 on acoustic shell), replace with a panel explicitly showing |delta^(2)| << |delta^(3)| and label as 'vanishes to machine precision'. (3) Evaluate analytically and plot the symbolic result.

color_palette_fixes.issue: Multi-platform curves rendered in black/monochrome. Should use project palette: steel blue #2E86AB (Steinhauer), berry #A23B72 (Heidelberg), amber #F18F01 (Trento).
color_palette_fixes.suggested_fix: Update visualization code to assign COLORS['steinhauer'], COLORS['heidelberg'], COLORS['trento'] to the respective platform traces.

color_palette_fixes.issue: Red/green color scheme may be inaccessible for color-blind readers (deuteranopia/protanopia).
color_palette_fixes.suggested_fix: Replace red/green with blue (#2E86AB) / amber (#F18F01) from the project palette, or use hatching patterns in addition to color.

color_palette_fixes.issue: Uses generic green/red for dispersive/dissipative. Missing legend on Heidelberg and Trento panels.
color_palette_fixes.suggested_fix: Add legend to all three panels. Consider using project palette colors for dispersive/dissipative.

cosmetic_suggestions.issue: Correction bars invisible due to 6+ order-of-magnitude dynamic range vs T_H.
cosmetic_suggestions.suggested_fix: Add inset panels or a second row showing only correction terms at zoomed scale.

cosmetic_suggestions.issue: X-axis labels slightly crowded for SO groups at small display sizes.
cosmetic_suggestions.suggested_fix: Rotate x-axis labels 45 degrees or increase figure width.
